title: daucus pumilus subsp. maritimus
intro: Daucus pumilus subsp. maritimus, also known as sea carrot, is a subspecies of the daucus pumilus plant, a perennial herb native to the coastal areas of Europe and North Africa. The medicinal properties of sea carrot include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its potential health benefits. The health benefits of sea carrot include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support immune function, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various health conditions. Sea carrot can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine as a poultice or compress to aid in wound healing and skin conditions.
